name: ultimate-browsing
tagline: Tiered escalation skill for blocked web pages: WAF-bypass fetchers, platform-native readers, then a stealth-Chrome fallback with local cooki
verdict: Well-structured, in-budget routing skill (141 lines) with clear triggers/anti-triggers. Two real risk points for installers: it decrypts locally-stored browser session cookies (Keychain/libsecret/DPAPI) for reuse — powerful and documented with 0600/no-commit caveats, but still credential-adjacent; and it auto-installs external tools (CloakBrowser, agent-brow

highlights.summary: scripts/extract_cookies.py reads and decrypts local browser cookies via macOS Keychain, Linux libsecret, or Windows DPAPI (lines 100-112) — session-credential material, even though scoped to local ext

Lines 40 and 87 state the engine 'auto-installs deps on first run' and CloakBrowser/agent-browser are 'runtime-installed tools (not vendored here)' with version pins deferred to references/chrome-stea
No explicit statement of what the agent should return to the user (extracted text shape, when to report failure vs. partial content) beyond mentioning an optional --json flag on the engine.
